name: arq (Async Redis Queue for Python)

auth.notes: Redis connection auth via Redis URL

performance.notes: Redis-backed; async-native; lower overhead than Celery

agent_readiness.notes: Async-first Redis task queue by the creator of Pydantic. Define tasks as plain async functions. WorkerSettings for queue config. ctx dict for shared resources (DB connections, HTTP sessions). Cron jobs. Job deduplication via job_id. Retry with exponential backoff. arq.create_pool() for enqueueing. await pool.enqueue_job('func', arg1). Results stored in Redis. Health check endpoint. More modern than Celery (async-native), simpler than Temporal. Best paired with FastAPI for async Python microservices.

gotchas: No built-in worker monitoring UI (unlike Celery Flower)
best_when: FastAPI/async Python apps needing background jobs
Teams wanting simpler Celery replacement for async tasks
Redis already in stack for caching
Cron jobs within async Python services
avoid_when: Complex workflow DAGs (use Temporal or Prefect)
No Redis available (use Huey with SQLite or RQ)
Sync Python codebase (Celery or Huey better)
summary: arq is an async-first Redis task queue by the Pydantic creator. Define jobs as plain async functions, enqueue with pool.enqueue_job(). WorkerSettings for config, ctx for shared resources (DB connections). Cron jobs, job deduplication, retry with backoff. Results in Redis. Best for FastAPI/async Python services wanting a simpler, async-native Celery replacement. Pairs well with Redis already used for caching.
